properties.label: d - Distance
properties.description: Distance of the drawing point from the center of the inner circle as a percentage of the buffer width. Controls how far the lobes extend outward.

properties.label: a - Animation
properties.description: Gradually changes the distance parameter (d) over time, causing the spirograph pattern to morph and evolve as it draws.

properties.label: Length
properties.description: How much of the spirograph curve is drawn each frame, measured in degrees. Higher values draw more of the pattern, creating a longer visible trail.

properties.label: Width
properties.description: Thickness of the drawn spirograph line in pixels. The line is drawn along the normal to the curve at each point.

properties.label: State Definition
properties.description: Selects which state definition to use from the model's configured state maps (e.g., SingleNode, NodeRange, or custom state definitions).
Choose between selecting a named state directly or driving states from a timing track. Each timing mark's label is matched to a state name.

properties.label: Mode
properties.description: Controls how timing track labels are interpreted. 'Default' uses labels as state names directly. 'Countdown' treats labels as numbers and counts down. 'Time Countdown' counts down from a time value (MM:SS). 'Number' displays digits for numeric values. 'Iterate' cycles through the listed states over the timing interval.

properties.label: Color
properties.description: Controls how palette colors are assigned to active states. 'Graduate' blends through the palette over time. 'Cycle' assigns a different palette color to each timing interval. 'Allocate' assigns a fixed palette color based on the state's index number.

properties.label: Fade(ms)
properties.description: Duration in milliseconds for states to fade in and out at the start and end of each timing interval. Set to 0 for instant on/off switching.

properties.label: Number Strobes
properties.description: How many strobe flashes are active at the same time. Higher values create a denser strobe field.

properties.label: Strobe Duration
properties.description: How many frames each strobe flash remains visible before fading out. Higher values make flashes linger longer.

properties.label: Strobe Type
properties.description: Shape of each strobe flash. Type 1 is a single pixel, type 2 adds a random cross arm, type 3 is a full plus shape, and type 4 alternates between plus and X shapes.

properties.label: Reacts to music
properties.description: When enabled, the number of active strobes scales with the current music volume level.

properties.label: Movement
properties.description: The path the tendril head follows. Options include geometric patterns like circle and square, zig-zag paths, music-reactive modes, or fully manual control via value curves.

properties.label: Tune Movement
properties.description: Controls the magnitude of the tendril head movement. Higher values produce larger, more dramatic movements across the buffer.

properties.label: Thickness
properties.description: The line width of the tendril in pixels. Higher values draw a thicker, more visible tendril strand.

properties.label: Friction
properties.description: Controls how quickly node velocity decays. Lower values allow nodes to move more freely, while higher values make the tendril more sluggish.

properties.label: Dampening
properties.description: How much each node is influenced by the velocity of its predecessor. Higher values make the tendril body follow the head more closely.

properties.label: Tension
properties.description: Controls how quickly the spring force decays along the tendril chain. Higher values keep the tail tighter to the head; lower values let it wave more freely.

properties.label: Trails
properties.description: Number of parallel tendril strands drawn. Each trail has a slightly different spring constant, creating a ribbon-like appearance.

properties.label: Length
properties.description: Number of nodes in the tendril chain. More nodes create a longer, more flowing tendril.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: How fast the tendril head moves along its chosen path. Higher values make the tendril traverse its pattern more quickly.

properties.label: Horizontal Offset
properties.description: Shifts the entire tendril pattern left or right as a percentage of the buffer width.

properties.label: Vertical Offset
properties.description: Shifts the entire tendril pattern up or down as a percentage of the buffer height.

properties.label: Manual X
properties.description: Manually sets the horizontal target position for the tendril head when movement is set to Manual. Use with a value curve to animate the path.

properties.label: Manual Y
properties.description: Manually sets the vertical target position for the tendril head when movement is set to Manual. Use with a value curve to animate the path.

properties.label: Text
properties.description: The text string to display. Supports multiple lines and special tokens for countdown modes. When non-empty, the file picker and lyric track are disabled.

properties.label: From File
properties.description: Path to a text file whose contents are displayed instead of the text field. Each line in the file is treated as a separate text line. Disabled when the Text field above is non-empty.

properties.label: From Lyrics
properties.description: Selects a lyric timing track to use as the text source. The current lyric phrase or word is displayed at each point in time. Disabled when Text or From File is set.

properties.label: Font
properties.description: The font face, size, and style used to render the text.

properties.label: XL Font
properties.description: Selects an xLights bitmap font (under fonts/) to use instead of the OS font. 'Use OS Fonts' falls back to the Font picker above.

properties.label: Movement
properties.description: Direction the text moves across the model. Options include scrolling left/right/up/down, diagonal, wavey motion, word-flip, and vector-based movement between start and end positions.

properties.label: Move to center
properties.description: When enabled, the text movement stops when the text reaches the center of the model rather than continuing off screen. Only meaningful when Movement is not 'none'.

properties.label: Do not repeat
properties.description: When enabled, the text scrolls through once and then stops instead of repeating in a loop. Only meaningful when Movement is not 'none'.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: How fast the text scrolls across the model. Higher values produce faster movement.

properties.label: Effect
properties.description: Text rendering style. Normal draws text horizontally; other options rotate or orient the text vertically.

properties.label: Count down
properties.description: Enables countdown mode. The text field value is used as the target, and the displayed text shows the remaining time in the selected format.

properties.label: Offsets In Pixels
properties.description: When enabled, the X and Y position values are interpreted as pixel offsets rather than percentage-based positions.

properties.label: Color Per Word
properties.description: When enabled, each word in the text is drawn using a different color from the palette, cycling through the available colors.

properties.label: X-axis Start
properties.description: Horizontal starting position of the text. Used as the origin for vector movement or as a static offset when movement is none.

properties.label: Y-axis Start
properties.description: Vertical starting position of the text. Used as the origin for vector movement or as a static offset when movement is none.

properties.label: X-axis End
properties.description: Horizontal ending position for vector movement mode. The text moves from the start position to this position over the effect duration.

properties.label: Y-axis End
properties.description: Vertical ending position for vector movement mode. The text moves from the start position to this position over the effect duration.

properties.label: Number Branches
properties.description: Number of horizontal branch rows on the tree. The buffer height is divided evenly among branches, with garland lights sweeping across each row.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: How fast the garland lights sweep across each branch row. Higher values make the animation cycle faster.

properties.label: Show Tree Lights
properties.description: When enabled, displays additional twinkling Christmas lights on the tree branches alongside the garland sweep animation.

properties.label: Percent of Lights
properties.description: Percentage of pixels that are actively twinkling at any given time. Higher values create a denser twinkling field.

properties.label: Twinkle Steps
properties.description: Number of frames for a complete twinkle cycle (fade up then fade down). Lower values create faster, snappier twinkles; higher values create slow, gentle fades.

properties.label: Style
properties.description: Selects the rendering algorithm. Old Render Method uses the original per-frame logic; New Render Method uses an updated approach with smoother transitions.

properties.label: Strobe
properties.description: When enabled, twinkling pixels snap on and off instantly instead of fading, creating a strobe-like flash effect.

properties.label: Re-Randomize after twinkle
properties.description: When enabled, each pixel is assigned a new random position after its twinkle cycle completes. When disabled, pixels twinkle in the same locations repeatedly.

properties.label: Sync to audio file
properties.description: When enabled, uses the sequence audio file as the video source and synchronises video playback to the audio timeline position. The picked video file, Start Time, Duration Treatment, and Speed are all overridden by the audio file's properties when this is on.
Video file selection: Select / Clear buttons, animated preview thumbnail, filename label, computed video duration, and a 'Match Effect To Video Duration' button.

properties.label: Start Time
properties.description: The timestamp in seconds within the video file to begin playback from. Allows skipping an intro or starting mid-video. Maximum updates dynamically once the picked video has been probed.
Read-only display of the picked video's total duration plus a 'Match Effect To Video Duration' button that resizes the effect on the timeline to match.

properties.label: Duration Treatment
properties.description: How the video duration maps to the effect duration. Normal plays at native speed, Loop repeats the video, Slow/Accelerate stretches or compresses playback to fit, and Manual allows explicit speed control.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: Playback speed multiplier used in Manual duration treatment mode. Negative values play the video in reverse. Use a value curve for acceleration effects.

properties.label: Maintain Aspect Ratio
properties.description: When enabled, the video is scaled to fit within the buffer while preserving its original aspect ratio, adding black bars if needed.

properties.label: Crop Left
properties.description: Percentage of the video frame to crop from the left edge. Use to remove unwanted portions of the video.

properties.label: Crop Right
properties.description: Percentage of the video frame visible from the left edge. Values less than 100 crop the right side of the video.

properties.label: Crop Top
properties.description: Percentage of the video frame visible from the bottom edge. Values less than 100 crop the top of the video.

properties.label: Crop Bottom
properties.description: Percentage of the video frame to crop from the bottom edge. Use to remove unwanted lower portions of the video.
Transparent Black checkbox with an inline brightness threshold slider (0-300). When enabled, video pixels below the threshold are keyed out as transparent.

properties.label: Sample spacing
properties.description: Pixel spacing used when sampling the video frame onto the model. A value of 0 uses default sampling; higher values skip pixels for a coarser look.

properties.label: Bars
properties.description: Number of bars or segments displayed in bar-based VU meter types. Controls the resolution of the frequency or level display.

properties.label: Type
properties.description: The visualization style for the VU meter. Options include spectrogram, volume bars, waveform, timing event responses, level-based displays, note tracking, and more.
options: Timing Event Timed Chase From Middle
Timing Event Timed Chase To Middle

properties.label: Timing Track
properties.description: The timing track used for timing-event-based VU meter types. Events on this track trigger visual responses like spikes, sweeps, and color changes.

properties.label: Filter Label
properties.description: Text filter applied to timing track labels. Only timing events whose labels match this filter text will trigger the VU meter response.

properties.label: Regex
properties.description: When enabled, the filter text is treated as a regular expression pattern instead of a plain text match.

properties.label: Sensitivity
properties.description: Audio level threshold that must be exceeded to trigger the VU meter response. Lower values make it more responsive to quiet sounds.

properties.label: Gain
properties.description: Amplifies or attenuates the audio signal level before it drives the visualization. Positive values boost the signal, negative values reduce it.

properties.label: Shape
properties.description: The shape used for level-shape VU meter type. The shape is drawn at a size proportional to the audio level.

properties.label: SVG File
properties.description: Path to an SVG file used as the shape when Shape is set to SVG. The SVG is rendered at a size driven by the audio level.

properties.label: Slow Down Falls
properties.description: When enabled, bars and levels fall gradually after a peak rather than dropping instantly, creating a smoother visual decay.

properties.label: Start Note
properties.description: The lowest MIDI note number included in frequency analysis. Used to focus the spectrogram or note display on a specific pitch range.

properties.label: End Note
properties.description: The highest MIDI note number included in frequency analysis. Together with Start Note, defines the visible pitch range.

properties.label: Logarithmic X axis
properties.description: When enabled, the frequency axis uses a logarithmic scale that better matches human pitch perception, giving more space to lower frequencies.

properties.label: Horizontal Offset
properties.description: Shifts the VU meter display left or right as a percentage of the buffer width.

properties.label: Vertical Offset
properties.description: Shifts the VU meter display up or down as a percentage of the buffer height.

properties.label: Audio Track
properties.description: Selects which audio track drives this VU Meter. 'Main' uses the sequence's primary audio; additional tracks are listed if alternate audio tracks have been added in Sequence Settings.

properties.label: Warp Type
properties.description: The distortion algorithm applied to pixels from lower layers. Options include water drops, dissolve, circle reveal, swirls, ripples, mirroring, copying, and flipping.

properties.label: Treatment
properties.description: Controls how the warp intensity changes over the effect duration. Constant maintains full distortion, In ramps from none to full, and Out ramps from full to none.

properties.label: X
properties.description: Horizontal position of the warp center point or focal area, as a percentage of the buffer width.

properties.label: Y
properties.description: Vertical position of the warp center point or focal area, as a percentage of the buffer height.

properties.label: Cycle Count
properties.description: Number of times the warp animation repeats over the effect duration. Higher values make the distortion cycle faster.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: Controls the rate at which the warp distortion animates. Higher values produce faster-moving distortion patterns.

properties.label: Frequency
properties.description: Controls the spatial frequency of the distortion pattern. Higher values create tighter, more closely spaced ripples or waves.

properties.label: Wave Type
properties.description: The waveform shape drawn across the model. Sine produces smooth curves, Triangle creates sharp zigzags, Square makes flat-topped waves, Decaying Sine fades over time, and Fractal/ivy generates random branching patterns.

properties.label: Fill Colors
properties.description: How the area below the wave is filled. None leaves it empty, Rainbow fills with a hue gradient, and Palette fills using the effect color palette.

properties.label: Mirror Wave
properties.description: When enabled, a mirrored copy of the wave is drawn on the opposite side, creating a symmetrical pattern around the center.

properties.label: Number of Waves
properties.description: Number of wave cycles drawn across the buffer width. 1.0 is one full wave; fractional values draw partial waves. Higher values fit more wave cycles onto the model.

properties.label: Thickness of Wave
properties.description: Vertical thickness of the wave line as a percentage of the buffer height. Higher values draw a wider band around the wave center.

properties.label: Scale Height of Wave
properties.description: Scales the vertical amplitude of the wave as a percentage. Lower values flatten the wave; higher values make it reach closer to the buffer edges.

properties.label: Speed
properties.description: How fast the wave scrolls horizontally across the model. Higher values create faster-moving waves.

properties.label: Wave Direction
properties.description: The horizontal direction the wave scrolls. Right to Left moves the pattern leftward, Left to Right moves it rightward.

properties.label: Y Offset
properties.description: Shifts the wave centerline up or down from the middle of the buffer. Positive values move the wave up, negative values move it down.
UI-only 'Reset panel when changing effects' checkbox, NOT serialized. Persisted to wxConfig under xLightsResetBufferPanel by the subclass.

properties.label: Render Style
properties.description: How the model's node layout is projected into a 2D pixel buffer. Populated dynamically from the model's supported styles.

properties.label: Buffer Stagger
properties.description: Percent offset applied to effect start time across nodes for a cascading appearance.

properties.label: Camera
properties.description: Camera to project the model through when BufferStyle is 'Per Preview'. Populated at runtime from 3D cameras in the preview setup.

properties.label: Transformation
properties.description: Fixed geometric transform applied to the buffer coordinate system before the effect renders.

properties.label: Blur
properties.description: Gaussian blur radius applied to the rendered buffer.
When enabled, the buffer is not cleared between frames — each frame composites onto the previous one.

Interactive sub-buffer rectangle picker (SubBufferPanel widget). Serialized as B_CUSTOM_SubBuffer. Built by the subclass because the widget instance is reused for live preview. 'growable: true' makes its tab-page row stretch to fill remaining vertical space, matching the legacy BufferPanel layout where the preview absorbed all available height.

Roto-Zoom preset dropdown: blank (idle), 'None - Reset', '1 Rev CW', '1 Rev CCW', 'Explode', 'Collapse', 'Explode + Spin CW', 'Shake', 'Spin CW Accelerate'. Blank is the resting state so the user can actively select 'None - Reset' to trigger a reset. NOT serialized — applies preset values to other sliders and resets selection to blank. UI-only.

properties.label: Z Rotation
properties.description: Static Z-axis rotation as a 0-100 percentage of a full turn (RZ_ROTATION_MAX). 100 = 360 degrees.

properties.label: Z Rotations
properties.description: Number of full Z-axis rotations over the effect duration. Stored as int; the engine divides by 10. settingPrefix:SLIDER because PixelBuffer reads SLIDER_Rotations as raw int and divides itself.

properties.label: Z Pivot Point X
properties.description: X coordinate of the Z-rotation pivot as a percentage of buffer width.

properties.label: Z Pivot Point Y
properties.description: Y coordinate of the Z-rotation pivot as a percentage of buffer height.

properties.label: Zoom
properties.description: Buffer zoom factor. Stored with divisor 10; 10 = 1.0x (no zoom), max 30 = 3.0x. settingPrefix:SLIDER because PixelBuffer reads SLIDER_Zoom as raw int and divides itself.

properties.label: Zoom Quality
properties.description: Number of interpolation samples used when zooming.

properties.label: X Rotation
properties.description: Fixed rotation around the X axis in degrees.

properties.label: X Pivot Point
properties.description: Pivot coordinate for X-axis rotation.

properties.label: Y Rotation
properties.description: Fixed rotation around the Y axis in degrees.

properties.label: Y Pivot Point
properties.description: Pivot coordinate for Y-axis rotation.

properties.label: Application Order
properties.description: Order in which X, Y, and Z rotations compose when any 3D rotation is non-zero.
UI-only 'Reset panel when changing effects' checkbox, persisted to wxConfig under xLightsResetColorPanel. NOT serialized with a C_ prefix. Placed at the top of the panel to match Buffer / Timing.

Compound top row: palette grid + shift/reverse buttons + recent-palettes dropdown + ☰ menu button. Built entirely by the subclass because the palette widgets are bespoke (ColorCurveButton + drag-drop targets + 4-row grid).
Compound chroma key row: 'Chroma Key' label + ChromaSensitivity slider + ChromaColour picker + Enable checkbox. Slider/picker are disabled when Enable is off; subclass handles the enable state.

Compound sparkles row: 'Sparkles' label + SparkleFrequency slider + VC button + text + lock + 'Sparkles reflect music' checkbox + SparklesColour picker + lock. Single-line compound to match legacy layout.

properties.label: Brightness
properties.description: HSV brightness multiplier as a percentage (100 = unchanged). Applied per-pixel as hsv.v *= brightness/100 at render time.

properties.label: Contrast
properties.description: S-curve contrast adjustment applied after brightness. 0 is no change.

'Brightness Level' checkbox. Built as a custom control because the legacy serialization key is 'CHECKBOXBRIGHTNESSLEVEL' (no underscore between CHECKBOX and BRIGHTNESSLEVEL) and the render engine reads that exact key.

properties.label: Hue
properties.description: HSV hue shift (stored -100..100, divided by 100 at render). Wraps around.

properties.label: Saturation
properties.description: HSV saturation shift. Clamped to [0,1] after adjustment.

properties.label: Value
properties.description: HSV value (lightness) shift. Clamped to [0,1] after adjustment.

UI-only 'Reset panel when changing effects' checkbox, NOT serialized. Persisted to wxConfig under xLightsResetTimingPanel by the subclass. Controls whether SetDefaultControls clears user values when the selected effect changes.

properties.label: Suppress Effect Until Frame
properties.description: Frame number before which the effect renders as black / invisible. Used to hold the layer off until a specific frame inside the effect's timespan.

properties.label: Freeze Effect At Frame
properties.description: Frame number at which the effect stops advancing. The last-rendered frame is held for the remainder of the effect's timespan. Default 999999 means 'never freeze'.

Custom compound row: Morph checkbox + Effect Layer Mix slider + lock button, matching the legacy layout. The Morph checkbox is backed by E_CHECKBOX_LayerMorph and the slider by E_SLIDER_EffectLayerMix (both legacy names preserved).
Custom row: Layer Method choice + '?' help button. Serialized as T_CHOICE_LayerMethod.

Custom row: Canvas checkbox + 'Layers ...' button. When Canvas is enabled and a layer range is available the button becomes usable and opens the LayerSelectDialog; the selected layer list is persisted as T_LayersSelected by the subclass.

Compound header row built by the subclass: [In_Transition_Type choice] + 'Time (s)' label + [Fadein BulkEditComboBox]. Matches the legacy single-line layout and keeps In_Transition_Type / Fadein addressable via the window walk under their legacy ID_CHOICE_In_Transition_Type / ID_TEXTCTRL_Fadein names. Suppress-if-default for these two controls is handled explicitly in TimingPanel::GetTimingString.

properties.label: Adjustment
properties.description: Generic tuning parameter passed to the selected in-transition (0-100). Meaning depends on the transition.

properties.label: Reverse
properties.description: Reverses the direction / polarity of the in-transition. Ignored by transitions that are inherently non-directional.
Compound header row built by the subclass: [Out_Transition_Type choice] + 'Time (s)' label + [Fadeout BulkEditComboBox]. Same single-line pattern as In_Transition_Header.

properties.label: Adjustment
properties.description: Generic tuning parameter passed to the selected out-transition (0-100).

properties.label: Reverse
properties.description: Reverses the direction / polarity of the out-transition.
